note: WorkRail cannot register live cron jobs. This file is a configuration document for the operator to configure monitoring alerts and calendar reminders based on the triggers listed here.
competitor_scope: AI code review ecosystem (CodeRabbit, PR-Agent/Qodo, Greptile, human review methodology, academic research)
primary_verdict: Ship three prompt-level improvements first (two-pass self-reflection, confidence constraint language, per-family finding cap) before any infrastructure investment. Validate 'review methodology enforcement' category frame with user research.

time_based_triggers.rationale: PR-Agent community is shipping fast. CodeRabbit changelogs update frequently. AI code review market is moving at quarterly cadence.
Pricing, community sentiment, and strategy signals change faster than product surface. Monthly spot-check on GitHub Issues, HN threads, pricing pages, and job postings.

event_based_triggers.event: PR-Agent ships MCP integration OR AGENTS.md support (PRs #2356, #2348, #2387)
event_based_triggers.rationale: When these ship, the PR-Agent community convergence threat becomes 'verified' instead of 'likely'. The entire threat read and differentiation story must be re-evaluated.

event_based_triggers.event: CodeRabbit pricing change or new plan tier
event_based_triggers.detection: Watch https://docs.coderabbit.ai/management/plans for changes. Archive current pricing page to Wayback Machine for diff.
event_based_triggers.rationale: CodeRabbit pricing at $48/dev/mo is the current data. A price decrease (or freemium model for full reviews) would materially change competitive dynamics.

event_based_triggers.event: CodeRabbit or Qodo funding round or acquisition
event_based_triggers.rationale: Significant funding would accelerate agentic exploration infrastructure and potentially lower pricing. Acquisition by GitHub/Microsoft/GitLab would change the competitive landscape entirely.

event_based_triggers.event: CodeRabbit major version release or marketing relaunch
event_based_triggers.rationale: A major version release may change the product surface enough to invalidate the current architecture inferences.

event_based_triggers.event: 3+ new independent user reviews on HN/Reddit in 30 days with a new complaint theme
event_based_triggers.detection: Monthly HN Algolia search: coderabbit OR pr-agent OR code-review-ai, filter last_30_days
event_based_triggers.rationale: User complaint themes are the primary signal for what users actually care about. A new theme may invalidate the current 'signal-to-noise is #1 complaint' finding.

event_based_triggers.event: Greptile publishes independent benchmark or user case study
event_based_triggers.detection: Watch greptile.com/blog for new technical posts
event_based_triggers.rationale: Greptile is currently [unverified-vendor-claim]. Independent evidence would upgrade their codebase-indexing claim to 'verified' and change the competitive gap analysis.

event_based_triggers.event: Team is about to make a roadmap decision this analysis was meant to inform
event_based_triggers.detection: Any sprint planning or roadmap meeting discussing wr.mr-review quality overhaul
event_based_triggers.rationale: Before acting on urgent ledger items, human reviewer should confirm verdicts. 08_human_signoff.md verdicts are proposed defaults, not confirmed decisions.

event_based_triggers.event: User research with 3+ high-risk engineering teams completed
event_based_triggers.rationale: The changes-positioning verdict ('validate methodology enforcement category frame') requires user research to resolve. When that research happens, re-evaluate whether the JTBD frame holds.

key_open_questions_for_next_run: Did PR-Agent ship MCP integration and AGENTS.md support? If yes, what did early adopters say about quality vs wr.mr-review?
Do users in high-risk engineering segments (fintech, healthcare, security-critical) recognize 'review methodology enforcement' as a job they need done?
Does the target segment already use CodeRabbit? If yes, is wr.mr-review an add-on (methodology layer on top of CodeRabbit) or a substitute?
What is Qodo 2.0's feature set? Is it now a credible competitor that was inaccessible during this research?
Did wr.mr-review ship the three urgent improvements (two-pass self-reflection, confidence constraints, volume caps)? What was the measured impact on finding quality?
